title: Bridge Essay: Is urban living good for mental health?

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Ecology/Animism** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Ecology/Animism — nature connectedness, biophilia, environmental neuroscience.
**Phase context:** Nature connectedness, biophilia, environmental neuroscience.

**Is urban living good for mental health?** — Curr Opin Psychiatry (2019). DOI: 10.1097/YCO.0000000000000489. PMID: 30695002. PURPOSE OF REVIEW: The questions of urban living, mental health and well-being are complex issues correlated to many interacting factors. The purpose of this review is to provide data on mental health challenges of urban life and perspectives to address these challenges. RECENT FINDINGS: Urbanization may cause mental health problems, such as psychotic experiences, depression and stress-related disorders, particularly in vulnerable individuals.
